title: Lesson 23: (Kana) - Lesson 23: (Katakana) "ア, イ, ウ, エ, オ"

intro_cards.title: Welcome to Katakana
intro_cards.body:
Katakana is the second Japanese syllabary. Every katakana character has the same sound as its hiragana counterpart:

ア = あ (a), イ = い (i), ウ = う (u)

Katakana is used for:
• Foreign loanwords (コーヒー, coffee)
• Onomatopoeia and sound effects
• Emphasis (similar to italics)
• Scientific and technical terms

The characters look more angular compared to hiragana's flowing curves.
